user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: DBS Sets the Peer Bar with Record Wealth Fees Before HSBC Reports Tomorrow
talking_points.why_it_matters: DBS posted record Q1 wealth fees of SGD907mn ahead of HSBC's results tomorrow morning, giving Adam's APAC change teams a live performance benchmark to close the gap against.
bullets: DBS wealth management fees hit a record SGD907mn in Q1, driven by investment product sales and bancassurance — on record total income of SGD5.95bn and net profit of SGD2.93bn, beating analyst consensus.

Total DBS wealth AUM reached SGD492bn (approx US$386bn) across Treasures, Private Client, and Private Bank — HSBC WPB's Q1 Asia NNA will be the direct comparison point when results drop at 12pm HKT tomorrow.
DBS raised its dividend to 81 cents per share, signalling the wealth-led strategy is delivering capital returns — the exact earnings model Adam's APAC change portfolio is built to validate.

source_links.title: Manila Times: DBS Q1 Beats, Outlook Flat

talking_points.headline: HSBC Q1 2026 Results Tomorrow 12pm HKT: WPB Wealth Momentum Is the APAC Read
talking_points.why_it_matters: Tomorrow's results test whether HSBC WPB's wealth fee income maintained its 20% YoY growth trajectory against a peer set — DBS and StanChart — that just reported record quarters, directly validating Adam's AI-enabled change programme.
bullets: Results at 5am BST / 12pm HKT on 5 May; consensus expects ~$13.68bn revenue and $0.31 EPS, with wealth fee income needing to extend the 20% YoY growth trend from the prior period.

IWPB attracted $80bn net new invested assets in the prior year — Q1 2026 Asia NNA will show whether HSBC WPB APAC held its lead against DBS (SGD492bn AUM) and StanChart's 34% wealth income surge last week.

The board will declare the first 2026 interim dividend; any commentary on AI-enabled RM productivity and digital transformation return is critical intelligence for the Q2 change portfolio and investment case.

talking_points.headline: MAS Cross-Bank AI for Scam Detection Establishes a New APAC AI Compliance Baseline
talking_points.why_it_matters: MAS's May 4 five-bank federated AI POV for financial crime sets a regulatory expectation for AI-driven compliance integration across APAC, expanding the scope of Adam's AI change programme in Singapore with a hard timeline.
bullets: MAS, GovTech, and Singapore Police Force launched a Proof-of-Value with five banks pooling hashed transaction data for AI/ML pre-emptive scam detection — Singapore's first cross-institution federated AI model for financial crime.

HSBC Singapore as a major bank will face pressure to join future expansions; Adam's AI programme must now incorporate regulatory-grade financial crime AI alongside client-facing tools, requiring change portfolio reprioritisation.

MAS signals scope will expand post-POV — a second wave of AI compliance obligations is forming for APAC banks by end-2026, overlapping directly with HSBC WPB's AI-enabled RM deployment and digitalisation timeline.

source_links.title: MAS: AI Financial Crime Industry POV
TechNode: MAS Cross-Bank AI for Scams

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS Q1 2026: SGD2.93bn Net Profit, Record Wealth Fees SGD907mn
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS posted Q1 2026 net profit of SGD2.93bn (up 1% YoY), beating consensus of SGD2.83bn, on record total income of SGD5.95bn. Wealth management fees hit a new high of SGD907mn driven by investment product sales and bancassurance, with total wealth AUM reaching SGD492bn. The board raised the quarterly dividend to 81 cents per share, signalling confidence in its wealth-led earnings model ahead of HSBC's own Q1 results on 5 May.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Singapore's DBS Keeps 2026 Outlook Unchanged as Q1 Profit Beats
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS Group maintained its full-year 2026 guidance unchanged after Q1 net profit beat estimates, with rate headwinds to NII declared largely mitigated by non-interest income growth. Non-interest income was the primary earnings driver, with wealth fees at SGD907mn and transaction services fees at a record SGD257mn. Deposit growth is now guided in the high-to-higher single-digit range for the full year.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 Earnings Preview: What to Watch on 5 May
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Holdings will announce Q1 2026 results at 5am BST / 12pm HKT on 5 May, followed by an analyst call at 7.45am BST. Consensus expects revenue of ~$13.68bn and EPS of $0.31, with wealth fee income projected to extend its 20% YoY growth trajectory from the prior period. The key APAC read is whether IWPB Asia NNA momentum was sustained relative to StanChart and DBS, which both reported record wealth quarters last week.

Regulatory & Markets.title: MAS and Five Banks Launch AI Proof-of-Value for Scam Detection
Regulatory & Markets.summary: MAS, the Government Technology Agency, and Singapore Police Force launched a Proof-of-Value with five banks to pool hashed transaction data for AI/ML pre-emptive scam detection, the first cross-institution federated AI model for financial crime in Singapore. Privacy-preserving one-way hashing ensures contributing banks cannot identify each other's account data while enabling shared intelligence. MAS plans to expand scope and sophistication after assessing POV effectiveness.

AI & Technology.title: AI in Finance and Banking: April 30 2026 Roundup
AI & Technology.summary: LLRX's fortnightly AI-in-banking roundup covers OpenAI's acquisition of personal finance startup Hiro Finance (its second fintech deal in months, signalling AI companies moving aggressively into financial advice), 26% of US consumers now using AI financial advice tools, and Wall Street firms including BofA, Citi, and Wells Fargo cutting headcount while reporting record profits as the structural AI-automation trade-off scales.

Operations & Change.title: Banking Operating Models 2026: From Pilot to Enterprise Architecture
Operations & Change.summary: Analysis of how banks must redesign core operating structures in 2026 beyond agile methodology, moving to AI-enabled decision-making, integrated governance, and end-to-end process automation. The shift is from innovation pilots to proving operating model change can be delivered safely at scale, with legacy complexity and fragmented data identified as the primary constraints to enterprise AI adoption.
